// Generated by `drift atomic run` for local-dev mode. Listens on $PORT
// (or :3000 by default), accepts a JSON body, calls the user's
// {{FUNC}}(body, req) handler, and returns the {status, message,
// payload, headers} envelope. Matches the deploy-mode server's call
// signature so user code is identical between dev and prod.
package main

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"io"
	"log"
	"net/http"
	"os"

	drift "github.com/ondrift/sdk/go"
)

// recoveryMiddleware turns a function panic into a 500 instead of a
// runtime crash — matches the slice runner's posture so dev exits
// behave like prod ones.
func recoveryMiddleware(next http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc {
	return func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
		defer func() {
			if rec := recover(); rec != nil {
				log.Printf("recovered from panic: %v", rec)
				http.Error(w, "internal server error", http.StatusInternalServerError)
			}
		}()
		next(w, r)
	}
}

func main() {
	port := os.Getenv("PORT")
	if port == "" {
		port = "3000"
	}

	mux := http.NewServeMux()
	mux.HandleFunc("/", recoveryMiddleware(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
		defer r.Body.Close()

		raw, _ := io.ReadAll(r.Body)

		// Build the same drift.Request the slice runner builds in prod.
		// Headers are flattened (slice runner only carries the first
		// value per header; we match).
		hdrs := make(map[string]string, len(r.Header))
		for k := range r.Header {
			hdrs[k] = r.Header.Get(k)
		}
		req := drift.Request{
			Method:  r.Method,
			Path:    r.URL.Path,
			Headers: hdrs,
			Query:   r.URL.RawQuery,
			Body:    raw,
		}
		// Path parameters arrive in the X-Drift-Params query header (set by
		// the platform's router from the matched route template). Decode and
		// attach so req.Params is populated the same way in production.
		if p := r.Header.Get("X-Drift-Params"); p != "" {
			req.Params = parseParams(p)
		}

		// Decode body into the typed RequestBody the user's function expects.
		var body RequestBody
		_ = json.Unmarshal(raw, &body)

		status, message, payload, headers := {{FUNC}}(body, req)

		// Mirror the prod envelope shape exactly.
		out := map[string]any{
			"status":  status,
			"message": message,
			"payload": payload,
		}
		if len(headers) > 0 {
			out["headers"] = headers
			for k, v := range headers {
				w.Header().Set(k, v)
			}
		}
		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
		w.WriteHeader(status)
		_ = json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(out)
	}))

	server := &http.Server{Addr: fmt.Sprintf(":%s", port), Handler: mux}
	log.Printf("🚀 Starting server on http://localhost:%s", port)
	log.Fatal(server.ListenAndServe())
}

// parseParams decodes the `key=value&key=value` form the front proxy
// uses for path-parameter delivery. Stdlib only.
func parseParams(s string) map[string]string {
	out := map[string]string{}
	for _, kv := range splitAmp(s) {
		eq := -1
		for i := 0; i < len(kv); i++ {
			if kv[i] == '=' {
				eq = i
				break
			}
		}
		if eq < 0 {
			continue
		}
		out[kv[:eq]] = unescape(kv[eq+1:])
	}
	return out
}

func splitAmp(s string) []string {
	var parts []string
	start := 0
	for i := 0; i < len(s); i++ {
		if s[i] == '&' {
			parts = append(parts, s[start:i])
			start = i + 1
		}
	}
	parts = append(parts, s[start:])
	return parts
}

func unescape(s string) string {
	out := make([]byte, 0, len(s))
	for i := 0; i < len(s); i++ {
		switch {
		case s[i] == '+':
			out = append(out, ' ')
		case s[i] == '%' && i+2 < len(s):
			h := func(c byte) byte {
				switch {
				case c >= '0' && c <= '9':
					return c - '0'
				case c >= 'a' && c <= 'f':
					return c - 'a' + 10
				case c >= 'A' && c <= 'F':
					return c - 'A' + 10
				}
				return 0
			}
			out = append(out, h(s[i+1])<<4|h(s[i+2]))
			i += 2
		default:
			out = append(out, s[i])
		}
	}
	return string(out)
}
